slices.focus: Defending concentrated outreach model with data evidence
slices.summary: Faced county budget pressure and defended the concentrated follow-through model using scoreboard data, callback logs, and a wall-mounted status map proving drop-off reduction. Successfully kept the model intact. Shifted from field executor to internal advocate for the approach. Decided to create a one-page standing summary document to institutionalize the argument so it doesn't need to be re-won each budget cycle.
open_loops: Budget pressure will return — whether the concentrated model is truly protected or just bought another round
How to institutionalize the argument beyond winning the room once with a single presentation
Whether the new role of internal defender is sustainable or an additional burden layered onto existing work
decisions: Create a one-page standing summary with conversion rates, drop-off rates, and follow-through touchpoints
Keep the wall status map and evidence logs current for rapid deployment when budget pressure returns
Frame the core argument as 'concentrated follow-through lowers drop-off and protects outcomes' using data not sentiment
